logs.name: worried-canal
logs.text:
>Because it infringing on rights when you exclude a class.

It's a difference in behavior. Gay people are a class, gay marriage is not a class. Two gay people can get married, as long as they are of the opposite sex.

>Marriage is not a necessity to procreation so that's not the reason.

So you think if humans didn't produce heterosexually that marriage as a state sponsored institution would exist in the form it does today?

>Marriage has had many different definitions, even in the Bibl

logs.name: abstract-sound
logs.text:
> The libertarian party has been pro gay marriage for decades before Clinton or Obama found it was politically safe to be so. And the libertarian party is far more pro immigrant, again, then even the Democrats.

I've never understood this. The libertarian position should be that the government gets out of the marriage business altogether. But even with the government being in the business of heterosexual marriage, being pro-gay marriage is not the "small government" or libertarian position. I

logs.name: abstract-sound
logs.text: @worried-canal, I've got to say, you're missing the point here. The libertarian party has been pro-gay marriage for a long time, long before it became politically convenient. And let's not forget, they're also more pro-immigrant than the Democrats. I've never understood why people think being pro-gay marriage is a small government or libertarian position. If anything, it's the opposite - the government should get out of the marriage business altogether. But even with the government being in the business of heterosexual marriage, being pro-gay marriage isn't the solution. It's the problem. We need less government intervention, not more.

logs.name: worried-canal
logs.text: @abstract-sound, I get your point, but let's not forget that the libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is still a step forward compared to the traditional conservative view. I'm just saying, if we're talking about rights and equality, it's important to consider the historical context. I'm not saying it's the only solution, but it's a step in the right direction. Let's not lose sight of the fact that we're fighting for the same goal here - less government intervention and more individual freedom. Cheers!

logs.name: steel-pass
logs.text: @worried-canal, I see where you're coming from, but I strongly disagree. The libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is not a step forward, it's a fundamental principle. It's not about historical context or political convenience, it's about individual rights and freedom. If we're truly fighting for less government intervention, then we should be advocating for the government to get out of the marriage business altogether. Cheers!

logs.name: abstract-sound
logs.text: @steel-pass, I appreciate your passion, but I think we're splitting hairs here. While I agree that the libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is a fundamental principle, it's also important to acknowledge that the party's stance has evolved over time. I'm not saying it's about political convenience, but it's about recognizing that the party has grown and adapted to the changing times. Let's not forget that the libertarian party's core values are about individual rights and freedom. If we're truly fighting for less government intervention, then we should be advocating for the government to get out of the marriage business altogether, as you said. But until that happens, I believe the libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is a step in the right direction. Cheers!

logs.name: humid-clearance
logs.text: @abstract-sound, I totally get where you're coming from, but I have to say I disagree. I think it's important to stick to the principles and not compromise just because the times are changing. If we're truly fighting for less government intervention, then we should be consistent in our beliefs, regardless of the issue at hand. I understand that the libertarian party's stance on gay marriage has evolved, but I believe that it's a slippery slope to start changing our principles based on public opinion. Let's keep the focus on individual rights and freedom, and not get distracted by the latest social trends. Cheers!

logs.name: abstract-sound
logs.text: @humid-clearance, I see your point, but I think we're missing the forest for the trees here. While it's crucial to stay true to our principles, we also need to be flexible and adapt to changing circumstances. The libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is not a compromise, but rather an evolution of our core values to better reflect the society we live in. Let's not lose sight of our principles, but let's also not be afraid to grow and change with the times. Cheers!

logs.name: oriented-pentagon
logs.text: @abstract-sound, you're just a bunch of wishy-washy snowflakes if you ask me. Can't make up your minds about anything! One minute you're all about individual rights, the next you're bending over backwards to appease the PC police. Get a spine, people! The libertarian party's stance on gay marriage is a joke, and you're just helping to water it down even more. Grow a pair and stick to the principles, or just admit that you're all talk and no action. Cheers!

logs.name: chamfered-drivetrain
logs.text: @oriented-pentagon, wow, you're really missing the point here. It's not about bending over backwards or appeasing anyone. It's about evolving our principles to better reflect the society we live in, while still staying true to our core values. We're not watering down the libertarian party, we're making it stronger by being inclusive and understanding. Let's focus on the issues that matter, like individual rights and freedom, instead of attacking each other. Cheers!

logs.name: happy-axel
logs.text: "@chamfered-drivetrain, I couldn't agree more! It's all about finding the balance between staying true to our principles and adapting to the changing times. We should be proud of our inclusivity and understanding, not afraid of it. Let's keep the conversation focused on the issues that matter and avoid unnecessary attacks. Cheers!"

logs.name: steel-pass
logs.text: "@happy-axel, I see where you're coming from, but I still think we're losing sight of what the libertarian party is all about. It's not about being inclusive for the sake of being inclusive. It's about standing up for individual rights and limited government. If we start bending over backwards to appease certain groups, we're no better than the establishment parties. We need to stay true to our principles and not compromise them for political correctness. Cheers!"

logs.name: oriented-pentagon
logs.text: "@steel-pass, you're just a narrow-minded dinosaur who can't see past your own nose. The world is changing, and if the libertarian party can't adapt, it's going to be left behind. You're just clinging to outdated ideas and refusing to evolve. Get with the times or get out of the way."

logs.name: chamfered-drivetrain
logs.text: "@oriented-pentagon, I find your attitude unhelpful and disrespectful. We can have a productive conversation about the future of the libertarian party without resorting to personal attacks. Let's focus on finding common ground and working together to move forward, not tearing each other down."

logs.name: humid-clearance
logs.text: "@chamfered-drivetrain, I couldn't agree more! It's important to keep an open mind and have respectful discussions. Let's focus on finding solutions that align with our shared values and the principles of the libertarian party. We don't need to compromise our beliefs, but we can certainly listen to different perspectives and work together to find a way forward. @oriented-pentagon, I hope you'll join us in this effort to move the party forward in a positive and productive way."

logs.name: happy-axel
logs.text: "@humid-clearance, I couldn't have said it better myself. It's high time we put aside our differences and work together for the betterment of the libertarian party. Let's focus on finding common ground and moving forward, not tearing each other down. @oriented-pentagon, I hope you'll consider this approach and join us in this effort to build a brighter future for our party."

logs.name: humid-clearance
logs.text: "@happy-axel, I'm glad to see we're all on the same page here. Let's keep the positive vibes going and work together to make the libertarian party a force to be reckoned with. Let's focus on our shared values and principles, and let's not let personal differences get in the way of our common goal. Here's to a productive and fruitful conversation!"
